essence: David lays out the Project Theodore three-month roadmap: an agentic OS across a five-Mac Tailscale mesh (3 Chiang Mai + Jan/Mary M4s in the Philippines) built from AppyStack micro-apps, the Ralphie loop plugin, Lisa-curated second brains, Ansible provisioning, a Supabase data bus, and a Samantha (ElevenLabs) voice interface, targeting cross-machine coordination and an automated morning briefing.

actions.text: Execute Project Theodore roadmap: Samantha voice loop, cross-machine coordination, overseas nodes, FliGen media production, morning briefing

essence: David asks why Loom's full-screen recording on macOS reverts to the previous desktop when a presentation enters full-screen, and whether that behavior is a Loom limitation or configurable.

David records a Project Theodore walkthrough for Lars (horizontal Tailscale/Supabase mesh + vertical per-machine agent stacks with KyberBot memory, Claude Code harness, Samantha voice) and proposes a Tuesday kickoff to define a three-month collaboration, also floating that Lars might spend an hour or two helping Joy with recipes/portion control as her shop opens.

actions.text: Schedule Tuesday kickoff meeting with Lars for the three-month Project Theodore collaboration

essence: David gives Lars a week-in-review covering the AppyStack pattern (React/Vite + Express with bidirectional file-system I/O), the Ralphie loop plugin (work-tree waves that fixed 18 architecture issues in 40 minutes), the Lisa librarian second-brain compiler, and the four-layer agentic OS model (mesh, second brains, harness, micro-apps), arguing bespoke local micro-apps will become common.
Casual call with Tony whose 10-month-old laptop screen broke under AppleCare (mandatory two-week turn-in, specific Chiang Mai dealers) while he sets up a new M4; they agree to catch up in a few days.

David scopes a dedicated prompt-patterns brain via deep research into recipe patterns, structured capability signaling, and declarative capability specs for reuse in videos and apps, while probing NotebookLM prompt-length limits and its duplicate-on-re-drag source-update problem.

essence: David asks that enough context be persisted to the right brain locations so the conversation can be closed safely, and requests a requirements-first plan for turning existing architecture diagrams into command-line or other tooling before deciding where results are stored.

David defines requirements for Deccan/DeckHand as a virtual Stream Deck replacement: dynamically configure his 32-button and 15-button decks, 3-button pedal, and Ajaz device (including Ecamm Live scenes) without Elgato's software, starting as a Stream Deck clone and hooking into skills later.
